channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: New CEO Steve O'Donnell vows to unite NASCAR and return the fun
item.description: Steve O'Donnell was introduced as the sanctioning body's chief executive officer at Talladega Superspeedway on Saturday and vowed to "make some moves" that will return the storied racing series to its roots.
item.content:encoded:
Steve O'Donnell was introduced as the sanctioning body's chief executive officer at Talladega Superspeedway on Saturday and vowed to "make some moves" that will return the storied racing series to its roots.

(Image credit: Mike McCarn)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Rocky Balboa statue takes up a new home inside Philly art museum
item.description: The bronze sculpture is on display inside the Philadelphia Museum of Art as part of a new exhibition on the impact and cultural importance of statues.
item.content:encoded:
The bronze sculpture is on display inside the Philadelphia Museum of Art as part of a new exhibition on the impact and cultural importance of statues.

(Image credit: Tassanee Vejpongsa)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: Roommate charged with two counts of murder in death, disappearance of two USF students
item.description: Authorities have filed murder charges against the roommate of a Bangladeshi doctoral student who disappeared with his girlfriend from the University of South Florida.
item.content:encoded:
Authorities have filed murder charges against the roommate of a Bangladeshi doctoral student who disappeared with his girlfriend from the University of South Florida.

(Image credit: Hillsborough County Sheriff's Office)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Pope Leo reiterates opposition to death penalty on same day U.S. approves firing squads
item.description: Pope Leo reiterated the Catholic Church's teaching that the death penalty is "inadmissible," in a video message released hours after the Justice Department said it would allow firing squads for federal executions.
item.content:encoded:
Pope Leo reiterated the Catholic Church's teaching that the death penalty is "inadmissible," in a video message released hours after the Justice Department said it would allow firing squads for federal executions.

(Image credit: Christopher Furlong)
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx

item.title: Justice Department makes it easier to deport those with DACA status
item.description: Three appellate immigration judges sided with Department of Homeland Security lawyers who appealed a decision from Immigration Judge Michael Pleters terminating removal proceedings for DACA recipient Catalina "Xóchitl" Santiago.
item.content:encoded:
Three appellate immigration judges sided with Department of Homeland Security lawyers who appealed a decision from Immigration Judge Michael Pleters terminating removal proceedings for DACA recipient Catalina "Xóchitl" Santiago.

(Image credit: Brendan Smialowski)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: DOJ wants to shield its lawyers from outside scrutiny. Critics worry about oversight
item.description: Critics say the proposed rule to let the DOJ step into state bar investigations could weaken one of the last independent checks on government lawyers.
item.content:encoded:
Critics say the proposed rule to let the DOJ step into state bar investigations could weaken one of the last independent checks on government lawyers.

(Image credit: Matt McClain)
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: Trump and Congress cut funding for Planned Parenthood. Can Botox keep it afloat?
item.description: After President Trump and Congress cut certain Medicaid funding to Planned Parenthood in last year's budget, some clinics have started offering aesthetic services, including Botox, to stay afloat.
item.content:encoded:
After President Trump and Congress cut certain Medicaid funding to Planned Parenthood in last year's budget, some clinics have started offering aesthetic services, including Botox, to stay afloat.

(Image credit: Tracy Barbutes for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Laura Fitzgerald

item.title: Iran's foreign minister leaves Pakistan, then Trump cancels U.S. delegation's travel
item.description: After arriving on Friday, Iran's Abbas Araghchi has left Islamabad, prompting President Trump to announce that his envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will no longer travel there Saturday for peace talks
item.content:encoded:
After arriving on Friday, Iran's Abbas Araghchi has left Islamabad, prompting President Trump to announce that his envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will no longer travel there Saturday for peace talks

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Justice Department to allow firing squads for executions in move to ramp up capital punishment
item.description: The Justice Department will adopt firing squad as a permitted method of execution as the Trump administration moves to ramp up and expedite capital punishment cases.
item.content:encoded:
The Justice Department will adopt firing squad as a permitted method of execution as the Trump administration moves to ramp up and expedite capital punishment cases.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Workers in Arizona don't have protections they need from triple-digit temperatures
item.description: Labor groups say Arizona isn't moving fast enough to establish new protections for people whose work exposes them to the state's sizzling high temperatures.
item.content:encoded: Labor groups say Arizona isn't moving fast enough to establish new protections for people whose work exposes them to the state's sizzling high temperatures.
item.dc:creator: Katherine Davis-Young
